title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure can disrupt critical business operations, compromise sensitive data, and result in significant financial losses due to downtime and ransom payments.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes.
Regularly back up critical cloud-based data and ensure backups are stored offline or in secure environments.
Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to identify and mitigate ransomware activity in real-time.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Dromara warm-flow up to 1.8.4. Impacted is the function...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Dromara warm-flow up to 1.8.4. Impacted is the function SpelHelper.parseExpression of the file /warm-flow/save-json of the component Workflow Definition Handler. The manipulation of the argument listenerPath/skipCondition/permissionFlag results in code injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote code injection via the manipulation of specific parameters in the Workflow Definition Handler, potentially enabling attackers to execute arbitrary code, compromise systems, and gain unauthorized access.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Dromara warm-flow to the latest patched version if available.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the affected parameters (listenerPath, skipCondition, permissionFlag) to prevent code injection.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection mechanisms to identify potential exploitation attempts.

The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Tenda F451 1.0.0.7 allows for remote exploitation via a stack-based buffer overflow in the fromAddressNat function. This could lead to unauthorized remote code execution, potentially compromising the device and any network it is connected to.

Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and consider disabling the affected function if a patch is not yet available.

title: A vulnerability was found in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. This issue affects the function...
description: A vulnerability was found in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. This issue affects the function install_plugin_upload of the file astrbot/dashboard/routes/plugin.py of the component install-upload Endpoint. The manipulation of the argument File results in sandbox issue. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in AstrBot allows remote attackers to exploit the install-upload endpoint, potentially leading to sandbox escape and unauthorized access to sensitive systems. This could result in data breaches, system compromise, or further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable or restrict access to the install-upload endpoint until a patch is available.
Monitor for any signs of exploitation, including unusual activity or unauthorized file uploads.
Contact AstrBotDevs for updates on the vulnerability and implement temporary mitigations, such as input validation or sandbox hardening.

title: A vulnerability was determined in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. Impacted is the function...
description: A vulnerability was determined in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. Impacted is the function add_mcp_server of the file astrbot/dashboard/routes/tools.py of the component MCP Endpoint. This manipulation of the argument command causes command injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in AstrBot up to version 4.22.1 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via command injection in the MCP Endpoint. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or full system compromise if exploited.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of AstrBot, if available. If no patch exists, consider disabling or restricting access to the vulnerable MCP Endpoint.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for the 'command' argument in the add_mcp_server function to mitigate the risk of command injection.
Monitor systems for signs of exploitation, such as unusual command execution or unauthorized access, and apply intrusion detection/prevention mechanisms.

description: A vulnerability was identified in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. The affected element is the function post_data.get of the component API Endpoint. Such manipulation leads to server-side request forgery. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in AstrBot up to version 4.22.1 allows attackers to exploit the API Endpoint via server-side request forgery (SSRF). This could lead to unauthorized access to internal systems, data exfiltration, or further exploitation of the infrastructure. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or patch AstrBot to a version newer than 4.22.1, if available. If no patch is available, consider disabling or restricting access to the vulnerable API Endpoint.
Implement input validation and sanitization on the post_data.get function to prevent malicious payloads from being processed.
Monitor for unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts targeting the API Endpoint, and review logs for signs of exploitation.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar forking in maybe_fork_scalars() for BPF_OR

maybe_fork_scalars() is called for both BPF_AND and BPF_OR when the
source operand is a constant.  When dst has signed range [-1, 0], it
forks the verifier state: the pushed path gets dst = 0, the current
path gets dst = -1.

For BPF_AND this is correct: 0 & K == 0.
For BPF_OR this is wrong:    0 | K == K, not 0.

The pushed path therefore tracks dst as 0 when

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31413.

description: A weakness has been identified in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. Affected is the function setRadvdCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. This manipulation of the argument maxRtrAdvInterval causes os command injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands via the CGI Handler, potentially leading to full system compromise, unauthorized access, data theft, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware of Totolink A7100RU devices to the latest version if a patch is available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify exploitation attempts.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.1. This impacts the...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.1. This impacts the function decode_image of the file metagpt/utils/common.py. The manipulation of the argument img_url_or_b64 results in server-side request forgery. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform server-side request forgery (SSRF), potentially enabling unauthorized access to internal systems, data exfiltration, or further exploitation of the affected server. Since the exploit is publicly available, the risk of widespread attacks is elevated.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or patch the FoundationAgents MetaGPT software to mitigate the vulnerability if a fix becomes available.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the 'img_url_or_b64' parameter to prevent malicious payloads from being processed.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity, particularly requests targeting internal systems, and deploy web application firewalls (WAFs) to block malicious traffic.

title: A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the ...
description: A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the `AppLollmsMessage` class in parisneo/lollms prior to version 2.2.0. The vulnerability arises from the lack of sanitization or HTML encoding of the `content` field when deserializing user-provided data. This allows an attacker to inject malicious HTML or JavaScript payloads, which can be executed in the context of another user's browser. Exploitation of this vulnerability can lead to account takeover, sessi

title: CVE-2026-5540 - A vulnerability has been found in code-projects Simple Laundry System 1.0. This vulnerability affect
description: A vulnerability has been found in code-projects Simple Laundry System 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /modifymember.php of the component Parameter Handler. Such manipulation of the argument firstName leads to sql injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute SQL injection attacks via the 'firstName' parameter in the /modifymember.php file. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, database manipulation, or complete system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied parameters, especially 'firstName'.
Update the Simple Laundry System to the latest patched version if available, or contact the vendor for a fix.
Implement web application firewalls (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

title: CVE-2026-5590 - A race condition during TCP connection teardown can cause tcp_recv() to operate on a connection that
description: A race condition during TCP connection teardown can cause tcp_recv() to operate on a connection that has already been released. If tcp_conn_search() returns NULL while processing a SYN packet, a NULL pointer derived from stale context data is passed to tcp_backlog_is_full() and dereferenced without validation, leading to a crash.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability exploits a race condition in TCP connection teardown, potentially leading to a crash or denial of service (DoS) in affected systems. This could disrupt network services and availability, particularly in environments with high traffic or critical infrastructure.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-5590.
Implement network-level monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify and mitigate unusual traffic patterns that could exploit this vulnerability.
Review and harden system configurations to minimize exposure, such as limiting unnecessary TCP connections and enabling rate-limiting where possible.
